vision: A mobile app that helps individuals manage their daily tasks through simple task creation, completion tracking, and organisation without the complexity of full project management tools.

epics.name: Task Creation & Management
epics.description: Core functionality allowing users to create, edit, and delete tasks to manage their daily to-do items

stories.title: Create a new task with title
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to create a new task with a title, so that I can capture items I need to do
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the task list screen When I tap the add task button and enter a title and confirm Then a new task appears in my task list
Given I am creating a new task When I leave the title empty and try to save Then the app prevents saving and shows a validation message
stories.notes: Foundation for all task management functionality

stories.title: Add optional due date to task
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to add an optional due date when creating a task, so that I can track when tasks need to be completed
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating a new task When I tap the due date field Then a date picker is displayed for selection
Given I am creating a task When I skip the due date field and save Then the task is created without a due date

stories.title: Edit an existing task
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to edit an existing task, so that I can correct mistakes or update task details
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an existing task When I tap on the task to edit and modify the title and save Then the task displays the updated title
Given I am editing a task with a due date When I change the due date and save Then the task displays the new due date
stories.notes: Assumed feature based on need to correct mistakes

stories.title: Delete a task from list
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to delete a task from my list, so that I can remove items that are no longer relevant
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a task in my list When I initiate the delete action and confirm Then the task is permanently removed from my list
Given I initiate a delete action When I am asked to confirm Then I can cancel and keep the task

epics.name: Task Completion
epics.description: Functionality to mark tasks as complete and track completion status

stories.title: Mark a task as complete
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to mark a task as complete, so that I can track my progress through my to-do list
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an incomplete task When I tap the completion checkbox or button Then the task is visually marked as complete
Given I have a completed task When I view my task list Then completed tasks are visually distinguished from incomplete tasks

stories.title: Unmark a completed task
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to unmark a completed task, so that I can restore it to incomplete if I made a mistake
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a completed task When I tap the completion indicator again Then the task returns to incomplete status
Given I unmark a completed task When I view the task list Then the task appears as a normal incomplete task
stories.notes: Natural extension of mark complete functionality

epics.name: Task List Display
epics.description: Viewing and displaying tasks in a clear, organised list format

stories.title: View all tasks in list
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to view all my tasks in a list format, so that I can see everything I need to do at a glance
acceptance_criteria: Given I have tasks saved When I open the app Then I see all my tasks displayed in a list
Given I have no tasks When I open the app Then I see an empty state message encouraging me to add tasks
stories.notes: Walking skeleton feature - basic list view

stories.title: Display task due dates in list
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to see due dates displayed on tasks in the list, so that I can quickly identify upcoming deadlines
acceptance_criteria: Given a task has a due date When I view the task list Then the due date is visible on the task item
Given a task has no due date When I view the task list Then no due date is shown for that task

epics.name: Task Filtering
epics.description: Filter tasks by completion status to focus on relevant items

stories.title: Filter to show incomplete tasks only
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to filter my task list to show only incomplete items, so that I can focus on what still needs to be done
acceptance_criteria: Given I have both complete and incomplete tasks When I apply the incomplete filter Then only incomplete tasks are displayed
Given I have applied the incomplete filter When I complete a task Then it is removed from the current view

stories.title: Filter to show completed tasks only
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to filter my task list to show only completed items, so that I can review what I have accomplished
acceptance_criteria: Given I have both complete and incomplete tasks When I apply the completed filter Then only completed tasks are displayed
Given I am viewing completed tasks When I unmark a task Then it is removed from the current view

stories.title: Clear filters to show all tasks
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to clear any active filters, so that I can return to viewing all my tasks
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a filter applied When I clear the filter Then all tasks are displayed regardless of completion status
Given I clear filters When I view the list Then both completed and incomplete tasks are visible

epics.name: Task Sorting
epics.description: Sort tasks by due date to prioritise work based on deadlines

stories.title: Sort tasks by due date ascending
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to sort my tasks by due date with earliest first, so that I can see my most urgent deadlines at the top
acceptance_criteria: Given I have tasks with various due dates When I sort by due date ascending Then tasks are ordered with earliest due dates first
Given I sort by due date When tasks have no due date Then those tasks appear at the end of the list

stories.title: Sort tasks by due date descending
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to sort my tasks by due date with latest first, so that I can see tasks with distant deadlines
acceptance_criteria: Given I have tasks with various due dates When I sort by due date descending Then tasks are ordered with latest due dates first
Given I sort by due date descending When tasks have no due date Then those tasks appear at the end of the list

epics.name: Data Persistence
epics.description: Store task data locally on device to ensure tasks persist between app sessions

stories.title: Persist tasks locally on device
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want my tasks to be saved on my device, so that they are still there when I close and reopen the app
acceptance_criteria: Given I have created tasks When I close the app and reopen it Then all my tasks are still present
Given I mark a task complete and close the app When I reopen the app Then the task completion status is preserved
stories.notes: Critical for basic app functionality - part of walking skeleton

stories.title: Load tasks quickly on app launch
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want my task list to load within 1 second, so that I can quickly check and add tasks without waiting
acceptance_criteria: Given I have up to 100 tasks saved When I launch the app Then the task list is fully displayed within 1 second
Given I open the app When tasks are loading Then I see a brief loading indicator if needed

epics.name: Cross-Platform Support
epics.description: Ensure the app works on both iOS and Android mobile platforms

stories.title: Run app on iOS devices
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to use the app on my iOS device, so that I can manage tasks on my iPhone or iPad
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an iOS device When I install and open the app Then all features function correctly
Given I am using iOS When I interact with the app Then the UI follows iOS design conventions
stories.notes: Platform setup as part of walking skeleton

stories.title: Run app on Android devices
stories.user_story: As an Individual User, I want to use the app on my Android device, so that I can manage tasks on my Android phone or tablet
acceptance_criteria: Given I have an Android device When I install and open the app Then all features function correctly
Given I am using Android When I interact with the app Then the UI follows Android design conventions
stories.notes: Platform setup as part of walking skeleton
